'''Sanyassa IV''' was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Moddell sector/Legends|Moddell sector]]. A [[Forest/Legends|forested]] world, Sanyassa was [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] to the barbaric [[Sanyassan/Legends|Sanyassan]] [[Species/Legends|species]], who were governed by a [[Kleptocracy/Legends|kleptocracy]]. The Sanyassans' technology was mostly on a feudal [[Technology level|level]] until they discovered others with spaceflight technology, which they stole and used for themselves, attacking [[Starship/Legends|starships]] all over the Moddell [[Sector/Legends|sector]].

==Description==
Sanyassa IV, also known simply as Sanyassa, was a stormy, terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]] occupying the fourth [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] position<ref name="EndorModdell" /> of the [[Outer Moddell systems]]' [[Sanyassa system/Legends|Sanyassa system]] in the [[Moddell sector/Legends|Moddell sector]], one of the [[Sector/Legends|subsectors]] making up the [[Inner Zuma Region]] within the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Zuma sector/Legends|Zuma sector]]. It lay on the [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] known as the [[Bakura Trace/Legends|Bakura Trace]], which connected it to the [[Endor system/Legends|Endor]] and [[Ast Kikorie system/Legends|Ast Kikorie systems]].<ref name="TEA" />

Sanyassa IV had a [[Atmosphere/Legends|Type I breathable atmosphere]] and standard [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]], and its climate was [[Temperature/Legends|temperate]]. The planet's terrain included [[Forest/Legends|forests]], [[Mountain/Legends|mountains]], rocky plains, and savannas, and it had 24-[[Standard hour/Legends|hour]] long [[Local day/Legends|local days]] and 389-day long [[Local year|years]].<ref name="EndorModdell" />

==History==
The history of the [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] [[Sanyassan/Legends|Sanyassan]] [[Species/Legends|species]] went back [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]] and hinted at a [[Time/Legends|time]] when their society was more advanced than what it came to be:<ref name="EndorModdell" /> a lawless world<ref name="TEA" /> where those who were strong came to power.<ref name="EndorModdell" /> The [[Kleptocracy/Legends|kleptocratic]] government existed during the times of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion]], and the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].<ref name="UAA" /> However, by [[3963 BBY]], at least one Sanyassan, [[Bar'injar]], had made his way beyond Sanyassa IV<ref name="KOTOR 45">[[Knights of the Old Republic 45|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 45]]</ref> and into the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]].<ref name="TEA Volgax">[[Knights of the Old Republic 45|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 45]] depicts [[Bar'injar]] being present on [[Volgax]], and ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' places the corresponding [[Volgax system/Legends|Volgax system]] in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]].</ref>

Although the Sanyassans occasionally pursued [[Farm/Legends|farming]] and limited trade on their home planet, they were more accustomed to war with [[Sword/Legends|blades]] and [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]].<ref name="EndorModdell" /> The world was fought over by the Sanyassans who lived there. A group of Sanyassans known as the [[Sanyassan Marauders|Marauders]] became shipwrecked on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]<ref name="TEA" /> at least one [[Standard century/Legends|century]] before [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]].<ref name="Date">{{GamerCite|9|Endor and the Moddell Sector}} states that [[Terak]] and the [[Sanyassan Marauders]] became shipwrecked on [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] at least a [[Standard century/Legends|century]] prior to Terak's [[Death/Legends|death]], as depicted in ''[[Ewoks: The Battle for Endor]]''. {{InsiderCite|23|Star Wars Publications Timeline|''Star Wars'' Publications Timeline}} establishes that ''The Battle for Endor'' occurs three [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the events of {{Film|IV}}, which corresponds to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> The [[Xenobiologist/Legends|xenobiologists]] of the New Republic would later debate over the origin of Endor's Marauders.<ref name="EndorModdell" />

==Inhabitants==
{{Main|Sanyassan/Legends}}
[[File:Terak-Chron.png|thumb|right|270px|Terak, one of the Sanyassans native to Sanyassa IV]]
Sanyassa was populated by one billion Sanyassans—a species of [[Leather/Legends|leathery]]-skinned [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] warriors—was governed by clans, and had no major imports or exports.<ref name="EndorModdell" /> The native Sanyassans included [[Terak]],<ref name="Terak">{{DB|character|terak|Terak}}</ref> [[Yavid]],<ref name="Yavid">{{DB|character|generalyavid|Yavid, General}}</ref> and [[Maygo/Legends|Maygo]]. The latter left Sanyassa as a [[Youngling/Legends|youth]].<ref name="Tessent">{{GamerCite|9|Race for the Tessent}}</ref> Some of the [[Ayrou/Legends|Ayrou]] species theorized that the Sanyassans had been particularly susceptible to the [[Din]] [[Supernova/Legends|supernova]] of [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]], which caused [[Radiation/Legends|radiation]] that they never recovered from. The Sanyassans spoke a [[Language/Legends|language]] of the [[Sanyassan (language)|same name]], and they used technology that was mostly on a<ref name="EndorModdell" /> primitive,<ref name="UAA" /> feudal [[Technology level|level]],<ref name="EndorModdell" /> although they did possess some [[Realspace/Legends|space]] technology that they had stolen from others.<ref name="UAA" />

The Sanyassans enjoyed using their newfound secrets of spaceflight to prey on the peaceful [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]] of Endor, who were quite frightened by the Marauders. Indeed, the Sanyassans used scavenged [[Starship/Legends|starships]] to attack anyone they wished in the Moddell sector, stealing new technology all the while. They were also hired out as thugs and [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguards]] by those who wished to take advantage of the Sanyassans' barbarous nature.<ref name="UAA" />

The Sanyassans had been causing trouble in the Outer Moddell [[Star system/Legends|systems]] for nearly as long as the neighboring [[Zorbian space pirates|Zorbians]], another band of [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] native to the nearby planet [[Zorbia II]].<ref name="EndorModdell" /> The Sanyassans were famous for their strength, temper, and lack of deep thought. Most of the Sanyassans on the planet longed to visit the [[Star/Legends|stars]].<ref name="UAA" />

==Behind the scenes==
Sanyassa IV was first mentioned in the [[Star Wars Gamer 9|ninth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Gamer]]'' magazine in two articles simultaneously: "[[Endor and the Moddell Sector]]" and "[[Race for the Tessent]]," both authored by [[Craig Robert Carey]], [[Daniel Wallace]], and [[Jason Fry]].<ref name="EndorModdell" /><ref name="Tessent" /> The magazine was published on [[February 22]], [[2002]].<ref name="GamerDate">{{SWArchive|url=gaming/other/rpg/news20020222.html|text=Starfighters Soar in Gamer #9}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the planet in grid square H-16.<ref name="TEA" />
